basics.name: Dianela Espinoza
basics.summary: Education economist specialising in impact evaluation of social programmes in developing countries. Experienced in rigorous econometric methods to evaluate large-scale education interventions. Completing an MPA in Data Science for Public Policy at LSE.

work.company: Group for the Analysis of Development (GRADE)
work.summary: Leading think tank in LATAM on social studies since 1980. Research Assistant for Alan Sanchez (University of Oxford) and Santiago Cueto (Young Lives Study).
highlights: Impact evaluation of Jornada Escolar Completa using R and Stata (submitted to Journal of Human Resources)
Data cleaning for Round 7 of the Young Lives Study in Peru
Led enumerator training for the History of Education module

work.summary: International research and policy advocacy organisation founded by Dean Karlan.
highlights: Supported theory of change development for evaluations on gender gaps in STEM and intercultural bilingual education
Programmed and supervised data collection through SurveyCTO
Led enumerator and auditor training on IRB compliance standards

education.institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
education.area: MPA in Data Science for Public Policy
courses: Supervised learning: Lasso/Ridge, logistic regression, decision trees, gradient boosting
Python: pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, xgboost, nltk, spacy

publications.name: Growing JUNTOS: Long-Term Impacts of Peru's Conditional Cash Transfer Programme on Human Capital
publications.publisher: Under review at Economic Development and Cultural Change
publications.summary: With Amanda Woodman and Alan Sanchez (University of Oxford). Evaluates long-term impacts of JUNTOS on human capital and family formation using propensity score matching on 2,000+ Young Lives participants.

publications.name: ¿Qué hemos aprendido del estudio longitudinal Niños del Milenio en el Perú? (2018-2025)
publications.publisher: GRADE, Group for the Analysis of Development
publications.summary: With Cueto, S., Rojas, V., Campos, A., & Curi, K. Led the education section.

publications.name: Education and Learning: Preliminary Findings from the 2023-24 Young Lives Survey (Round 7): Peru
publications.publisher: Young Lives Factsheet, University of Oxford
publications.summary: With Cueto, S. Led the writing of the education outcomes section.

presentations.name: Growing JUNTOS: Long-Term Impacts of Peru's Conditional Cash Transfer Programme on Human Capital
presentations.event: IX National Seminar, Peruvian Society of Educational Research (SIEP)
presentations.description: Presented preliminary results of the Growing JUNTOS working paper.
